description.en: The aim of the experiment is to Study the efficiency of single phase transformer by load test
The Instructions provide step-by-step guidance on how to perform the simulation
To use this feature, click on the “AI Guide” button. The AI guide will assist you in performing the simulation accurately at each step.
The CHECK button verifies all the connections to ensure that they are correct.
The AUTO CONNECT button makes all the connections automatically.
The ADD button adds the readings to the observation table.
The PLOT button helps visualise the readings through a graph.
The RESET button refresh the simulation to its initial state for a fresh start.
The PRINT button allows you to print the simulation.

The MCB acts as the main power switch for the experiment. It also protects the circuit by automatically disconnecting the power supply in case of an overload or short circuit. When turned ON, it supplies electrical power to the circuit.
The primary AC voltmeter measures the voltage applied to the transformer's primary winding. It helps ensure that the transformer operates at its rated input voltage throughout the load test.

The primary ammeter measures the current drawn by the transformer from the AC supply. Its readings increase with the applied load and help monitor the transformer's input current during the experiment.

The AC wattmeter measures the real power consumed by the transformer at the input side. It provides the input power reading, which is used along with the output power to determine the transformer's efficiency.

The autotransformer is used to provide a smooth and adjustable AC voltage to the primary winding of the transformer. It allows the input voltage to be increased gradually up to the rated value, preventing sudden voltage surges and enabling accurate testing.

The single-phase transformer is the main device under test, responsible for transferring electrical energy from the primary to the secondary winding through electromagnetic induction. Its performance under different loading conditions is analyzed to determine efficiency and voltage regulation.

The lamp load acts as a variable resistive load connected to the transformer's secondary side. By switching different lamps ON or OFF, the load is increased in steps, allowing the transformer to be tested under various operating conditions.
The secondary AC voltmeter measures the output voltage of the transformer. It is used to observe the voltage variation as the load changes, which is essential for calculating voltage regulation.
The secondary ammeter measures the current supplied by the transformer to the load. It indicates the output current at different load conditions and is required for calculating the output power.
Every ADD action records one row here. Add the no-load row first, then one row after each lamp-load switch.

This graph shows how the transformer's efficiency varies with output power. The efficiency increases with load and gradually reaches a high value near the rated output, indicating efficient operation.

title.en: Voltage Regulation vs Output Power Graph

description.en: This graph shows the variation of voltage regulation with output power. As the load increases, the voltage regulation also increases due to the voltage drop in the transformer windings.
After all readings are recorded and the graph is plotted, use this button to generate the report.
